intent.problem: The camera-refit decision is spread across unfold.ts: paint() bundles the 'toggleExpand' case with 'reveal'/'hide' and calls render(true), render() then runs reframeToFit() (L1557), which recomputes viewXform.k/x/y to fit ALL content — the same fit math is also copied verbatim in fitView() (L979). Nothing pure decides 'does THIS repaint move the camera', so 'a group toggle must not reframe' cannot be a behavioural claim, only a DOM side effect no acceptance case can reach.
intent.approach: Add one pure, dependency-free function ufFitXform(action: string, firstPaint: boolean, prev, content, stage, pad) -> { x, y, k } in src/panel/unfold/unfold-camera.ts (the ufSliceTargets/ufVerbAllowed/ufEscAction precedent: no ctx, no DOM, importable by the acceptance runner). Policy: refit when firstPaint OR action is one of reveal/hide/foldAll (the verbs that change which content is on screen); otherwise — crucially for toggleExpand — return prev unchanged so zoom and pan are preserved. When it does refit, it computes the identical clamped fit (k = clamp(min((stage.w-2pad)/content.w, (stage.h-2pad)/content.h), 0.15, 1.15); x centred; y = max(pad, centred)) that fitView/reframeToFit compute today, so the two hand-copied blocks collapse into this single resolver.
intent.rationale: E2/H1 factor-to-pure: 'toggling a group moves neither zoom nor focus' becomes a red-then-green projection acceptance claim (the returned transform equals the prior one), and the fit math has one owner instead of two drifting copies. Deterministic, no DOM — exactly the projection pattern acceptance.mjs documents for ctx/viewport-bound logic.

fm.name: ufFitXform
fm.description: pure camera resolver for one unfold repaint: the first paint and the visible-set verbs (reveal / hide / foldAll) fit content to the stage (clamped k in [0.15,1.15], centred, y floored at pad); every other repaint — crucially toggleExpand — returns the prior transform untouched so a group fold/unfold moves neither zoom nor focus. Owns the fit math previously duplicated in fitView and reframeToFit; no ctx, no DOM

interfaces.name: ufFitXform
accepts: prev: { x: number; y: number; k: number }
returns: { x: number; y: number; k: number }

cases.name: toggling a group open or closed moves neither zoom nor focus
revealing a hidden node refits content to the stage, ignoring the prior transform
the first paint always fits, even when the action is a toggle
a refit never zooms past the 1.15 readable ceiling

intent.problem: reframeToFit today recomputes the full fit transform and is fired by render() on every structural repaint — including a group toggle, whose paint() case (bundled with reveal/hide) calls render(true). That is the exact code path that throws a zoomed-in user back to a full-canvas zoom-out when they close a parent group. Its own fm description ('after every structural change the world transform-scales so all visible content fits') is now the drift to correct.
intent.approach: Route render()'s camera step through the pure ufFitXform(action.type, firstFit, viewXform, contentSize(), { width, height } of the stage, 64): assign the returned {x,y,k} to viewXform and apply the DOM transform (the existing anim/anim2 class + ~950ms cleanup stays here — presentation only). reframeToFit keeps its animated-apply role but its fit MATH is now the shared resolver, and because ufFitXform returns the prior transform for a toggleExpand, closing/opening a group repaints in place with no camera move. reveal/hide/foldAll and the first paint still fit exactly as before. No signature change: reframeToFit stays () => void.
intent.rationale: The behavioural contract lives in ufFitXform (proven by projection acceptance); this change is the dumb wiring — a single owner for the fit math and a repaint that consults it instead of unconditionally reframing on a toggle.

fm.name: reframeToFit
fm.description: apply the camera transform resolved by the pure ufFitXform for a structural repaint, animated ~900ms expo ease — a fit only for the first paint and the visible-set verbs (reveal / hide / foldAll); a group toggle (toggleExpand) resolves to the prior transform, so folding/unfolding moves neither zoom nor focus

phases.title: Pure camera resolver
phases.subtitle: ufFitXform (acceptance red first) — first-paint + visible-set verbs fit, every other repaint (toggleExpand) keeps the current transform

phases.title: Toggle repaints without a reframe
phases.subtitle: render resolves its transform through ufFitXform; reframeToFit's fit math is the pure resolver and a group toggle no longer fires a reframe
